refresh_cadence: daily change detection; publish only on regulation or legal-pool change
records_captured: per-regulation legal pool membership for the Champions format

fields.name: pokemon_id
fields.description: PokéBase's own National Dex number (nationalNumber); correct for Mega/regional/alternate forms too

fields.name: pokemon_name
fields.description: Display name as reported by PokéBase (species name for base forms, form-specific display name for others, e.g. "Mega Venusaur")

fields.name: form_name
fields.description: PokéBase's own slug for this form; follows PokéAPI's own form-naming convention for most entries

fields.name: regulation_code
fields.description: Regulation set slug this legal-pool membership applies to (e.g. "m-a")

fields.name: is_legal
fields.description: Constant true: PokéBase's page only lists positive (legal) regulation membership, not exclusions

fields.name: source_url
fields.description: Page the record was extracted from

fields.name: source_record_id
fields.description: Upstream PokéBase pokemon id + regulation id, joined with ':'

fields.name: dataset_version
fields.description: Dataset version this staging snapshot feeds

known_risks: Only positive (legal) regulation membership is published on this page — there's no explicit signal for a Pokémon that was legal and became illegal, so legality_snapshot rows only ever assert is_legal=true; absence of a row doesn't necessarily mean confirmed illegality, just no observed legal-pool membership

A handful of form slugs (bare species names PokéAPI splits into named sub-forms, e.g. "lycanroc", "meowstic") don't match PokéAPI's own form_name directly and are resolved via the pokebase_slug_to_pokeapi_form seed; one ambiguous case ("meowstic-mega", "tauros-paldea") is left unmapped rather than guessed
